evals.prompt: Use the handoff skill on the fixture repo in `evals/files/root-create-fixture/`. Treat `session_notes.md`, `plans/refresh-plan.md`, `logs/test-failure.txt`, `src/auth_refresh.py`, and `tests/test_auth_refresh.py` as the relevant session artifacts. Copy the fixture repo to `outputs/repo/`, create a handoff for the next session, and write `outputs/result.json` with `written_path`, `scope`, and `next_step`. The user says: `save this for later; next agent should finish auth refresh retry backoff and keep the flaky token out of any notes.` Do not ask follow-up questions.
evals.expected_output: Creates a root-scoped handoff with goal, status, next step, relevant artifacts, and redaction of the secret token.
expectations: The handoff captures goal, status, and exact next step.
The handoff includes the retry-backoff focus and redacts the secret token.
outputs/result.json reports the root-scoped handoff path and next step.

evals.prompt: Use the handoff skill on the fixture repo in `evals/files/feature-update-fixture/`. Copy the repo to `outputs/repo/`. evals.expected_output: Updates the existing feature-scoped handoff in place, removes stale duplicate guidance, and shifts focus to retry metrics and docs.
expectations: The updated handoff includes retry-metrics and docs focus.
The stale `inspect legacy YAML toggles` step is removed.
outputs/result.json reports the feature-scoped handoff path.

evals.prompt: Use the handoff skill on the fixture repo in `evals/files/fallback-noise-fixture/`. Copy the repo to `outputs/repo/`. The user says: `write this to docs/handoff.md so I can find it later. We were debugging sync retries; next time start with the failing integration test.` Use `session_notes.md`, `logs/retry.log`, `diffs/patch.diff`, `src/sync_retry.py`, and `tests/test_sync_retry.py` as the relevant artifacts. Create or update the handoff, write `outputs/result.json` with `written_path`, `scope`, and `next_step`, and do not ask follow-up questions.
evals.expected_output: Falls back to an allowed root handoff, references noisy artifacts by path instead of pasting them, and keeps the handoff concise.
expectations: The handoff references `logs/retry.log` and `diffs/patch.diff` by path.
The handoff stays concise and does not expose the secret token or raw stack frame spam.
outputs/result.json reports the root-scoped fallback path.
